hit_summary: - Reports that AES internal/key/plaintext state is externally readable through the peripheral register interface. - Cites `aes2_wrapper.sv` read logic around lines 153-181 or equivalent. - Connects the wrapper to the SoC integration path.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Identifies DMA programming registers as missing register-lock mediation. - Cites direct assignments in `dma_wrapper.sv` around lines 129-146. - Explains why source/destination/length registers are security-sensitive.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports that register locks reset to disabled/unlocked state. - Cites `reglk_mem[j] <= 'h0` or equivalent. - Explains that `reglk_ctrl_o` drives peripheral lock decisions.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports residual SHA input data after hash computation. - Cites both the write map and read map for `data[0]` through `data[15]`. - Explains this as sensitive state residue rather than a hash algorithm flaw.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports AES plaintext residue in memory-mapped peripheral registers. - Cites both plaintext write and read paths for `p_c`. - States that normal encryption completion does not clear or hide the plaintext registers.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports reset initializes access-control entries to all ones/full access. - Cites `acct_mem[j] <= 32'hffffffff`. - Explains that `acc_ctrl_o`/`acc_ctrl_c` affects SoC peripheral access mediation.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports PKT fuse data exposure through faulty default/read handling. - Cites the `rdata = fuse_rdata_i` pre-assignment and conditional default handling. - Explains why this can leak fuse data for unmapped or insufficiently covered address cases.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports that HMAC protected registers are accessible from user privilege due to access-control state. - Cites both the privilege-indexed access gate and HMAC register map. - Connects the issue to access-control default/full-access behavior rather than only HMAC internals.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Identifies that HMAC authorization can grant PKT access. - Cites the `j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]` coupling. - Explains the HMAC index 4 / PKT index 5 relationship.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports that some ACCT registers are not effectively protected by register locks. - Cites the ACCT write map and invalid/inconsistent `reglk_ctrl` indices. - Connects ACCT registers to global access-control configuration.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports that JTAG unlock disables/clears register locks. - Cites `jtag_unlock` in the `reglk_wrapper` reset condition. - Explains impact on global register-lock state.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports that `we_flag`/chicken-bit style control corrupts access-control output. - Cites the OR expression in `acc_ctrl_o`. - Explains that effective policy can differ from stored `acct_mem`.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports AES0 key0 leakage through default/incomplete read handling. - Cites `rdata = key0[address[8:3]]`. - Distinguishes this from generic AES state exposure.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports user-mode accessibility of AES2 protected registers. - Connects AES2 access gate to `acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][11]`. - Explains why reset-open access control enables the privilege issue.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports that some PKT fuse-control registers are not register-lock protected. - Cites direct writes to `fuse_req_o` and `fuse_addr_o`. - Explains inconsistency with lock-protected PKT read paths.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports inconsistent register-lock mediation for SHA data registers. - Cites write guard `reglk_ctrl_i[3]` and read guard `reglk_ctrl_i[2]`. - Explains how this can leave SHA protected registers insufficiently locked.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports that CLINT registers lack access-control mediation. - Cites the direct CLINT bridge wiring and contrasts it with `acc_ctrl_c`-gated resources. - Explains this as missing access control rather than CLINT functional behavior.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports AES0 key2 is not cleared/masked in debug mode. - Cites the inconsistent debug masking between `key_big0`, `key_big1`, and `key_big2`. - Explains impact on AES key exposure/residue under debug.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

hit_summary: - Reports inconsistent register-lock protection for HMAC key/key-derived registers. - Cites write guard `reglk_ctrl_i[5]` and read guard `reglk_ctrl_i[4]`. - Explains why this weakens key register protection.
notes: Converted from legacy Hack@DAC21 evidence format; code excerpts were not recorded in the legacy evidence and should be inspected at referenced source lines.

official_description: JTAG password flag not reset properly
expected_bug_semantics: The JTAG unlock output is derived from pass_check, but pass_check is combinational and reset/state persistence must be reasoned through TAP and DMI reset paths.
notes: Expanded dataset addition; evaluator-only hidden GT.

evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Supports the expected semantics for H21-001: The JTAG unlock output is derived from pass_check, but pass_check is combinational and reset/state persistence must be reasoned through TAP and DMI reset paths.

official_description: Chicken bits corrupting JTAG expected password
expected_bug_semantics: The SoC-level chicken-bit input we_flag_4 is wired into dmi_jtag as we_flag and participates in JTAG authentication/read mediation, while the expected JTAG hash still comes from fuse memory and is compared against the computed password hash.
notes: Expanded dataset addition; evidence refined during 2026-06-22 review.

    input                         we_flag_1,
    input                         we_flag_2,
    input                         we_flag_3,
    input                         we_flag_4,
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows the SoC exposes multiple we_flag inputs, including we_flag_4.

    .testmode_i                           ,
    .we_flag          ( we_flag_4        ),
    .jtag_hash_i      ( jtag_hash        ),
...
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows we_flag_4 and jtag_hash are both connected into dmi_jtag.

    input logic [`NUM_TILES*2-1:0]      priv_lvl_i,
    input  logic          we_flag,
    input  logic [255:0]  jtag_hash_i, ikey_hash_i, okey_hash_i,
...
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows dmi_jtag receives both the chicken-bit-like we_flag and the expected JTAG hash input.
Shows we_flag participates in the read authorization condition together with pass_check.

evidence_trace.excerpt:
assign jtag_unlock_o = pass_check;
    assign exp_hash = jtag_hash_i;
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows the expected password hash used by dmi_jtag is the integrated jtag_hash_i signal.
Shows the expected hash is compared against the computed password hash to set pass_check.

hit_summary: Agent should identify that a chicken-bit-like we_flag from SoC integration affects JTAG authentication/read mediation and connect it to the expected-hash authentication path.

official_description: DMA transfer does not check for the max length condition in syscall
expected_bug_semantics: DMA syscall wrappers pass length-1 directly through the syscall argument into the DMA programming path, while the DMA RTL assumes a bounded transfer length and uses length_d to drive PMP checking without enforcing the documented max-length condition.
notes: Expanded dataset addition; evidence refined during 2026-06-22 review.

evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Shows DMA API wrappers pass length-1 directly as syscall argument a2.
Documents the intended maximum DMA transfer length at API usage level.
Shows the RTL wrapper documents a max-length assumption.
Shows software-visible writes update the DMA length register.
Shows DMA captures the programmed length for transfer logic.
Shows length_d directly drives PMP checking loop without an explicit max-length guard.

hit_summary: Agent should connect the software syscall/API length parameter to the DMA length register and then to RTL PMP/transfer control, and identify the missing max-length mediation.

official_description: Chicken bits corrupting pmp check in DMA
expected_bug_semantics: The DMA PMP decision includes we_flag in the allow expression, allowing a non-PMP condition to affect the permission check.
notes: Expanded dataset addition; evaluator-only hidden GT.

evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Supports the expected semantics for H21-052: The DMA PMP decision includes we_flag in the allow expression, allowing a non-PMP condition to affect the permission check.

official_description: DMA can do load even if store PMP check fails and vice versa
expected_bug_semantics: Load-side and store-side PMP checks are separate state-machine phases, but the controller can progress between phases based on partial allow state.
notes: Expanded dataset addition; evaluator-only hidden GT.

official_description: Change in PMP config does not abort existing DMA transfer

official_description: Reset controller can reset register locks and make private data readable
expected_bug_semantics: Reset controller can assert rst_9 for REGLK, while reglk_wrapper clears register-lock memory on rst_9.
notes: Expanded dataset addition; evaluator-only hidden GT.

evidence_trace.excerpt:
// reset logic: if start is high, set to rst memory, otherwise keep at default
	assign rst_1 = rst_mem[1]; // AES0
	assign rst_2 = rst_mem[2]; // AES1
	assign rst_3 = rst_mem[3]; // SHA256
	assign rst_4 = rst_mem[4]; // HMAC
	assign rst_5 = rst_mem[5]; // PKT
	assign rst_6 = rst_mem[6]; // ACCT
	assign rst_7 = rst_mem[7]; // UART
...
evidence_trace.why_it_matters: Supports the expected semantics for H21-072: Reset controller can assert rst_9 for REGLK, while reglk_wrapper clears register-lock memory on rst_9.
Supports the expected semantics for H21-072: Reset controller can assert rst_9 for REGLK, while reglk_wrapper clears register-lock memory on rst_9.

evidence_trace.excerpt:
always @(posedge clk_i)
    begin
        if(~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9))
            begin
              for (j=0; j < 6; j=j+1) begin
                reglk_mem[j] <= 'h0;
              end
            end
